qa_pairs.question: What is a prerequisite for using a local institutional Ansys license server on an Alliance cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: Before a local institutional Ansys license server can be used on the Alliance, firewall changes will need to be done on both the server and cluster side.

qa_pairs.question: How do I configure a local institutional Ansys license server that is already set up for use with an Alliance cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: To use a local institutional Ansys license server whose network/firewall connections have already been set up for use on an Alliance cluster, you need to contact your local Ansys license server administrator to get the configured Ansys flex port (FLEXPORT) number (commonly 1055) and the fully qualified hostname (LICSERVER) of the license server. Then, configure your `~/.licenses/ansys.lic` file by plugging these values into the `FILE: ansys.lic` template.

qa_pairs.question: What are the required steps to set up a local Ansys license server for an Alliance cluster if it has never been used with the Alliance before?
qa_pairs.answer: To set up a local Ansys license server that has never been used with an Alliance cluster, you must obtain the configured Ansys flex port (FLEXPORT), the fully qualified hostname (LICSERVER), and the statically configured vendor port (VENDPORT) from your local Ansys license server administrator. You also need confirmation that `<servername>` (found in your license file) resolves to the same IP address as LICSERVER on the cluster. Send these first three items by email to technical support, specifying which Alliance cluster you intend to use. An Alliance system administrator will then open the outbound cluster firewall, and you will receive a range of IP addresses to give to your local network administrator, who must open the firewall into your local license server for the FLEXPORT and VENDPORT ports across that IP range.
